# rustybox

> A free-range, non-GMO fork of BusyBox written entirely in Rust. One
> fully-static binary, dual-arch (x86_64 + aarch64), memory-safe applets on
> uutils/ripgrep/walkdir/RustCrypto/tar/flate2/xz2, and `timeout`/`xargs`/`watch`/`flock` built in for agentic harnesses.
> Dual editions: GPL-2.0 (rustybox full) & 100% MIT (rustybox-core).

## What it is

- One fully-static musl binary — drop into a container `FROM scratch`, a
  rescue image, or a sandbox. No libc, no dependencies.
- 100% parity across all permissive applets: coreutils, ripgrep engine for
  grep, walkdir for find, RustCrypto hashes, tar/gzip/bzip2/xz archives.
- **rustybox-core (MIT edition)**: Compiles zero GPL code; 85+ memory-safe
  applets distributed under the MIT license.
- Built for coding agents: `timeout`, `xargs`, `watch`, `flock`, `dd`, `shuf`
  baked in, the userland an autonomous harness needs to bound and script work.
- Resurrected from a six-year-dead c2rust transpile.

## Install

```
# Grab rustybox-core (MIT edition) or rustybox (Full GPL edition)
curl -LO https://github.com/peterlodri-sec/rustybox/releases/latest/download/rustybox-core-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
chmod +x rustybox-core-* && ./rustybox-core-* --version
```
